text.text:
Dr, Elsaba, Office Manager Suelie, Office Manger Diana
I\n Have recordings of these conversation that I would look to provide to somebody who is higher than them.
Horrible accommodation
did not provide essential service.
Canceled by prior appointment. Because staff was away. They rescheduled me for my appointment only to schedule the wrong procedure. Because they scheduled me the wrong procedure, they had to rebook me for a consultation. Because a lady on that day, in October didn't have enough time.  So I had to wait.A month I informed the office manager that if she books me for that appointment I Maybe late, she said no worries.We will accommodate fast forward to today.I arrived an hour late and was told.I need to be rebooked.I I spoke with Office manager Suelie
Who could not provide much help I insisted with speaking with the consultation manager because nobody can verify office manager. Diana's conversation with me, which should have been noted if there are a Doctor's office. Conversations should be noted situations like that should be noted. And yet my conversation with office manager Diana was not noted. I was rescheduled to tomorrow at 12 PM. Another inconvenience this hospital does not communicate lax communication.  Horrible communication

text.text: An amazing group of student dentists and professors who manage all your dental needs in one building. My daughter and I have been patients here for years..from endodontics to oral surgery to orthodontics to general care. I have never failed to be well taken of. The building is very modern, clean, and the admin staff are very efficient. The only thing is it’s hard to get into the system. Going to the emergency clinic usually gets you going. The emergency clinic is open M-F, so if you have something drastic going on.. go there and you will be seen.  I’ve never had an issue whether early in the am or in the afternoon! It’s also amazing to watch the student dentists grow and know your dental care is helping them to become full fledged dentists. They are always, and I mean always, supervised by an attending expert/top notch professor, so you know you’re getting as good or better care than most private practices in the city. Can’t beat that but you do have to understand that it’s a dental school so some treatments may take longer as you go through a process of “checks and balances”. Love this place if you haven’t already figured that out ❤️‍🩹

text.text:
I've spent quite a few years getting my dentures. With the students at NYU and had no complaints, they're very compassionate and affectionate, and I love the way they detailed my dentures.
It's so advanced now that every tooth you can pick the pontic  for your dentures.
As well as the shade color of the gums, i have nothing bad to say about that school.
As a matter of fact, I'm inspired to go back to school to be a dental assistant & hygenist, because that was my trade for 20 years to go back there.

text.text:
Bianca Williams supported the harmful behavior of a university employee and, as a result, prevented me from completing my treatment. I need to ensure that Bianca and the team understand my attorney’s position on this matter, as outlined below.

Dear Dean Bertolami, Associate Dean Glickman, Ms. Williams and Esteemed Colleagues:

I am writing to formally notify you that I have consulted with my attorney, Mr. Michel Jr., regarding a serious incident that occurred during my visit to NYU Dentistry. I am in possession of evidence identifying the employee who verbally abused me and whose conduct made me feel unsafe as a patient.

As a patient, I am legally entitled to receive care in an environment that is safe, respectful, and free from intimidation, harassment, or abuse. Any conduct that compromises patient safety or violates this fundamental right constitutes a serious breach of professional and institutional obligations.

Please be advised that I formally dispute any charges for services associated with an encounter in which my right to patient safety was violated.

Should I receive any billing related to this incident, I will initiate legal action against the University without further notice. In that event, I request that NYU’s legal counsel contact me directly regarding this matter.

This correspondence serves as formal notice of my position. I expect this issue to be addressed promptly and resolved appropriately.

Sincerely,

Denise Thorne

text.text:
Well, I’m giving them one star because at least they did manage to pull my brothers teeth out.  The last year I’ve been helping my brother, who has some challenges, to manage his care . He has been in need of false teeth teeth which nyu dental made  for him, Be aware if you come here, you will be fitted by a student, and there is minimal oversight. The students come and go and the supervision changes and if you have a problem, you will be blamed.
If you are at all used to a private dentist  who actually treats you with respect, this is not the place to come. I witnessed my brother a subjected To insults and indignities by even the supervising dentist. Be assure that the “customers always right “does not apply here.
My brother now has dentures that were reworked and relined and patched again and still do not fit him correctly.  He unable to eat solid food but there is no care about that.  They would rather kick him the street, then remake a pair of dentures, even if you are willing to pay an additional portion.
The dentist that is now in charge has accused my brother of lying, and just pretending that he has tried to make these dentures work.
I think it is because my brother is a lower income, formally homeless person, and is not treated with care compassion someone  would expect from a medical professional .
What we’ve come to conclude is that they use their patients as guinea pigs. And frankly, I think guinea pigs might be treated better.
